title: tetrabaculum cacatua
side_effects: Tetrabaculum cacatua can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing severe allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and swelling, particularly in individuals with a history of sensitivities to certain plant extracts. In some cases, excessive consumption of the plant's leaves or roots can lead to gastrointestinal problems, such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Long-term use of tetrabaculum cacatua without proper guidance can also cause an imbalance in the body's electrolyte levels, resulting in muscle weakness, fatigue, and tremors. Furthermore, improper preparation or dosing of the plant's extracts can lead to interactions with other medications, exacerbating pre-existing conditions or causing unforeseen complications.
